entities.name: Prompt Engineering Literature Review
observations: Systematic review of 5 papers on prompt engineering for LLM reasoning
Papers cover chain-of-thought prompting, self-consistency, auto-CoT, zero-shot-CoT, and tree of thoughts
All papers focus on improving reasoning capabilities of large language models
Key themes: step-by-step reasoning, sampling diversity, automation of demonstrations, zero-shot approaches, tree-based exploration

entities.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
observations: Paper ID: 2201.11903 by Jason Wei et al.
Published at NeurIPS 2022 with 6500 citations
Few-shot prompting with intermediate reasoning steps
Works on arithmetic, commonsense, and symbolic reasoning tasks

entities.name: Self-Consistency
observations: Paper ID: 2203.11171 by Xuezhi Wang et al.
Published at ICLR 2023 with 3200 citations
Samples multiple reasoning paths and uses majority voting
Improves CoT accuracy without additional training

entities.name: Auto-CoT
observations: Paper ID: 2210.03493 by Zhuosheng Zhang et al.
Published at ICLR 2023 with 1800 citations
Automatically constructs CoT demonstrations using clustering and zero-shot CoT
Eliminates need for manual demonstration design

entities.name: Zero-shot-CoT
observations: Paper ID: 2205.11916 by Takeshi Kojima et al.
Published at NeurIPS 2022 with 4100 citations
Uses 'Let's think step by step' prompt without few-shot examples
Two-stage prompting: elicit reasoning then extract answer

entities.name: Tree of Thoughts
observations: Paper ID: 2305.10601 by Shunyu Yao et al.
Published at NeurIPS 2023 with 2400 citations
Generalizes CoT with tree-based exploration using BFS/DFS
Enables backtracking and deliberate planning for complex tasks
